name: The Farmers State Bank Of Waupaca
description_short: Locally owned, independent community bank serving Waupaca, Wild Rose, and Fremont, Wisconsin since 1911. Offers checking, savings, loans, and mortgages.
description_long:
Farmers State Bank of Waupaca was established on January 11, 1911, and opened for business on January 14, 1911, in downtown Waupaca. The bank has maintained its status as a locally owned and independent community bank for over 113 years, serving the Waupaca, Wild Rose, and Fremont areas. It is one of only three banks in Wisconsin that remained open during the Great Depression, reflecting its institutional stability and community commitment.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of consumer and business financial services. Their consumer offerings include checking and savings accounts, CDs, personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, home equity loans, and secured checking with identity theft protection and credit monitoring features. For business customers, they provide small business loans and commercial banking services. The bank has expanded from a single downtown location to multiple branches across the service area, though it has consolidated from five locations to three following changes in customer banking patterns and adoption of mobile banking.

Farmers State Bank distinguishes itself through its commitment to local ownership and community reinvestment. Leadership continuity and long-term employee relationships are highlighted as key to their success. The bank explicitly reinvests its money to support local schools, industrial development, and business development. With a mission statement focused on "working together with our customers and community as an independent bank by providing quality financial services in a friendly manner," they emphasize personalized service and community integration over corporate structure.

The bank is upgrading its digital infrastructure, with new online banking coming in April 2026. While they maintain competitive auto loan offerings and claim some of the best rates in the area, specifics on loan terms, fees, and comparative pricing are not detailed on their website. For consumers seeking traditional banking with local decision-making and community focus, this institution may be suitable, though comparison with larger regional banks on specific rates and fees would be prudent. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Secure checking with identity theft protection
pros: Established local institution with 113+ years of continuous operation (founded 1911)
One of only three Wisconsin banks that remained solvent during the Great Depression
Locally owned and independent — not part of a larger corporate bank holding company
Multiple branch locations across three communities (Waupaca, Wild Rose, Fremont)
Comprehensive product offering including checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, and business loans
Identity theft protection and credit monitoring bundled with secure checking account
Strong community reinvestment commitment in schools, industrial development, and local business
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to three communities in Wisconsin
No specific information on interest rates, APRs, or loan terms published on website
Upgrading online banking platform (downtime scheduled for April 2026 transition)
Reduced physical footprint — closed two branches (Churchill Street in 2006, King Branch in 2019)
No information on fee structure, minimum balances, or account requirements
best_for: Residents of Waupaca, Wild Rose, or Fremont seeking relationship-based community banking
Local business owners needing small business loans with local decision-making
Customers prioritizing local ownership and community reinvestment over national bank chains
Homebuyers and mortgage seekers in the tri-community service area
diagnosis: Farmers State Bank is best for residents and small business owners in the Waupaca, Wild Rose, and Fremont areas who value local ownership, community relationship banking, and personalized service. The main caveat is that specific loan rates, terms, and fee information are not available on their website, requiring direct contact to compare competitiveness against regional alternatives before committing.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
